steps.title: Identify the pattern to complete
steps.content: The text establishes that the same element has a lower ablation rate in slower-moving dust (SPC, AST) than in faster-moving dust (HTC, OCC). The example already gives iron from AST at 28%. The completion must show iron from a faster-moving source at a higher rate to complete the contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate three, confirm one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'iron from SPC dust is 20%.': SPC is also slow-moving dust. Comparing two slow sources does not illustrate the speed-vs-ablation pattern. ✗
  • Choice B — 'sodium from OCC dust is 100%.': Different element (sodium, not iron); the example requires the same element across sources. ✗
  • Choice C — 'iron from HTC dust is 90%.': Same element (iron), faster-moving source (HTC), higher ablation rate (90% vs 28%). This directly completes the contrast the example is building. ✓
  • Choice D — 'sodium from AST dust is 75%.': Different element and a slow-moving source already used in the first half — fails on both counts. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
